zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda Peche於1208年出生在英國德比夏爾波勒斯同時，她的父親Sir Nicholas Peche25歲，母親Alice Sifrewast24歲。她於1242年5月之前與Oliver Deincourt在英國英格蘭德比夏爾結婚。她去世於1283年11月25日在她的家鄉享年75歲。

nameMeanings.name: Peche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a variant of   Pech   ‘pitch, tar’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Peče or of its Germanized form Petsche , which is also a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname.

variants.name: Pech
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Manuel, Alvaro, Diego, Edelmira, Emilio, Francisco, Gilberto, Jorge, Jose Angel.  German Gerhard, Hans, Ilse, Lutz, Manfred, Wolf.

Czech and Slovak; Sorbian (Pěch and P'ech): from a pet form of the personal name Petr (Czech), Pětš (Lower Sorbian), and Peter (Slovak). This is also a Germanized form of the Upper Sorbian cognate Pjech.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German pech (German Pech) ‘pitch, tar’, presumably a nickname for someone with exceptionally dark skin or hair, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who prepared, sold, or used pitch. This may in part also be the origin of a homonymic Polish, Czech, and Slovak surname (compare above and below).

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Peche was born in 1208, in Boylestone, Derbyshire, England, her father, Sir Nicholas Peche, was 25 and her mother, Alice Sifrewast, was 24. She married Oliver Deincourt before May 1242, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. She died on 25 November 1283, in her hometown, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda Peche est née en 1208, à Boylestone, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Nicholas Peche, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Alice Sifrewast, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Oliver Deincourt avant mai 1242, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée le 25 novembre 1283, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 75 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda Peche è nata il nel 1208 nel Boylestone, Derbyshire, England, sua padre, Sir Nicholas Peche, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Alice Sifrewast, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Oliver Deincourt prima del maggio 1242, nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 25 novembre 1283, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 75 anni.

ko.Draycott in the Moors, Staffordshire, En: 영국 스태퍼드실르 드라이컷 인 테 무르스
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Peche(이)가 1208년 영국 더비실르 보일레스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Nicholas Peche(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Sifrewast(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1242년 5월 이전 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 Oliver Deincourt(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1283년 11월 25일 그녀의 고향에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda Peche nasceu em 1208, em Boylestone, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Nicholas Peche, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Alice Sifrewast, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Oliver Deincourt antes maio de 1242, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela faleceu em 25 de novembro de 1283, em sua cidade natal, com 75 anos.

ru.Draycott in the Moors, Staffordshire, En: дреикотт ин т мурс, стаффордшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda Peche родилась в 1208 г. в местности боилестоун, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Nicholas Peche было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Sifrewast, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Oliver Deincourt перед май 1242 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла 25 ноября 1283 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda Peche nació en 1208, en Boylestone, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Nicholas Peche, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Alice Sifrewast, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Oliver Deincourt antes de mayo de 1242, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 25 de noviembre de 1283, en su pueblo, a la edad de 75 años.
